mission.title: traefik: enable custom plugins/middlewares for Traefik
mission.description: enable custom plugins/middlewares for Traefik. Requested by 128+ users.

steps.title: Upgrade to Traefik v2.3 or later
steps.description:
Ensure you are running Traefik v2.3+, which introduced the Yaegi-based plugin system:
```bash
traefik version
```
If using Helm:
```bash
helm upgrade traefik traefik/traefik --set image.tag=v2.10
```

steps.title: Browse the Traefik Plugin Catalog
steps.description: Visit the Traefik Plugin Catalog at https://plugins.traefik.io to find community and official plugins. Each plugin page shows installation instructions and configuration options.

steps.title: Install a plugin via static configuration
steps.description:
Add the plugin to your Traefik static configuration (traefik.yml):
```yaml
experimental:
  plugins:
    my-plugin:
      moduleName: github.com/traefik/plugindemo
      version: v0.2.1
```
Traefik downloads and loads the plugin at startup using the Yaegi Go interpreter.

steps.title: Use the plugin as middleware
steps.description:
Reference the installed plugin as middleware in your dynamic configuration:
```yaml
http:
  middlewares:
    my-plugin-middleware:
      plugin:
        my-plugin:
          headers:
            X-Custom: "value"
  routers:
    my-router:
      rule: "Host(`example.com`)"
      middlewares:
        - my-plugin-middleware
      service: my-service
```
Restart Traefik and verify the middleware is active.

resolution.summary: Traefik implemented a plugin system using Yaegi (an interpreted Go runtime) instead of compiled Go plugins. Starting with Traefik v2.3, users can install community plugins from the Traefik Plugin Catalog (https://plugins.traefik.io). Plugins are loaded at runtime without recompilation.
codeSnippets:
# traefik.yml - Install a plugin from the catalog
experimental:
  plugins:
    my-plugin:
      moduleName: github.com/traefik/plugindemo
      version: v0.2.1

# Use the plugin as middleware in a route
http:
  middlewares:
    my-plugin-middleware:
      plugin:
        my-plugin:
          headers:
            X-Custom: "value"
  routers:
    my-router:
      rule: "Host(`example.com`)"
      middlewares:
        - my-plugin-middleware
      service: my-service

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with traefik installed or the issue environment reproducible.
